terms.definition: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy — upper GI endoscopy examining esophagus, stomach, and duodenum.
Endoscopic Retrograde Cholangiopancreatography — procedure to diagnose and treat bile duct and pancreatic duct conditions.
Endoscopic Ultrasound — endoscopy combined with ultrasound to image GI wall layers and adjacent structures.
Fine Needle Aspiration — sampling cells from a mass using a thin needle under EUS guidance.
Fine Needle Biopsy — sampling a core tissue specimen using a needle under EUS guidance.
Endobronchial Ultrasound — bronchoscopy with ultrasound to visualize mediastinal lymph nodes.
High-Level Disinfection — disinfection process that kills all microorganisms except large numbers of bacterial spores.
Automated Endoscope Reprocessor — machine that automates the HLD cycle for endoscopes.
Common Bile Duct — the duct that carries bile from the liver and gallbladder to the duodenum.
Common Hepatic Duct — duct formed by the union of left and right hepatic ducts.
Gastroesophageal Junction — the point where the esophagus meets the stomach.
Endoscopic Mucosal Resection — technique for removing large flat polyps from the GI tract.
Endoscopic Submucosal Dissection — advanced technique for en bloc resection of GI lesions.
Argon Plasma Coagulation — non-contact thermal treatment using ionized argon gas for hemostasis.
Percutaneous Endoscopic Gastrostomy — endoscopically placed feeding tube through the abdominal wall into the stomach.
